# Features — Well Production Monitoring & Allocation
**Module**: well-production  
**Generated by**: AIKit — Review Kit (@review-stories, mode: generate-features)  
**Date**: 2026-04-25  
**Total Features**: 28

---

## EPIC-001: Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration

### FEAT-001: PI Historian Real-time Ingestion
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: The system polls the OSIsoft PI Historian via PI Web API (REST) every minute for all configured well-level production tags, stores readings in Azure Data Explorer, and buffers data locally for up to 8 hours during outages.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-001, NFR-010, WF-001  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All configured well tags polled at configurable interval (default 1 minute)
- [ ] Tag readings written to ADX WellProduction database within 5 seconds of poll
- [ ] 8-hour local buffer retains data across ingestion service restarts
- [ ] Buffer replays to ADX without gaps on service restore
- [ ] Poll interval configurable per well group without service restart  
**Dependencies**: None

### FEAT-002: DeltaV OPC-UA Ingestion (Bravo Platform)
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: The system connects to Emerson DeltaV via OPC-UA for the Bravo offshore platform, ingesting production tags on the same 1-minute schedule as PI, with unified gap detection.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-001, WF-001, NFR-5.4  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] OPC-UA subscription established to DeltaV server on Bravo platform
- [ ] Tag readings written to the same ADX store as PI data with source tagged "DELTAV"
- [ ] Connectivity loss to DeltaV triggers same gap detection logic as PI (FR-003)
- [ ] IT Ops can configure OPC-UA endpoint without code change  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001 (ADX store and gap detection shared)

### FEAT-003: Data Gap Detection & Back-fill
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: The system continuously monitors ingested tag timestamps, classifies gaps by severity (< 15 min / 15 min–4h / > 4h), applies back-fill or raises Communication Loss events, and provides a resolution workflow for Production Engineers.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-003, BR-007, BR-008, WF-001  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Gaps ≤ 15 min: no action, normal processing
- [ ] Gaps 15 min–4h: back-filled with last-known-good value, flagged ESTIMATED in all outputs
- [ ] Gaps > 4h: Communication Loss event created, well locked from allocation until resolved
- [ ] Production Engineer notified automatically of Communication Loss events
- [ ] Communication Loss event resolution (approve/reject) unlocks well for allocation  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001, FEAT-002

### FEAT-004: Manual Production Data Entry
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: Provides a web screen for field operators to enter daily production readings for non-SCADA Permian wells and submit LACT tank gauge dip readings, with all manual values automatically flagged as ESTIMATED.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-002, FR-004, BR-015, WF-002  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Manual entry screen shows only wells assigned to the operator's field scope
- [ ] All manually entered values stored with ESTIMATED flag visible in all reports and exports
- [ ] LACT gauge dip entry available once per 24h cycle; system enforces single entry per well per day
- [ ] LACT vs separator discrepancy alert triggered if difference > ±1% (clarification Q-B4)
- [ ] Incomplete submission rejected with field-level validation errors  
**Dependencies**: EPIC-007 / FEAT-026 (auth + role scoping)

### FEAT-005: SAP PM Integration
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: Daily batch integration with SAP PM that imports maintenance notifications, work orders, and planned workover schedules into WPMA, and exports downtime events linked to SAP functional locations.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: NFR-5.4 (SAP PM), clarification Q-D1  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] SAP inbound batch runs at 02:00 UTC daily; imports PM01 notifications and PM02 work orders
- [ ] SAP work order numbers visible on well timeline alongside downtime events
- [ ] WPMA outbound batch runs at 03:00 UTC; exports planned maintenance and workover downtime events to SAP PM functional locations
- [ ] Integration failure alerts IT Ops via Azure Monitor; WPMA continues operating independently  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001

### FEAT-006: Snowflake Daily Production Export
**Epic**: EPIC-001 — Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration  
**Description**: Daily scheduled ELT job extracts finalized well-level production aggregates, allocation records, and downtime summaries from WPMA and loads them into the company Snowflake data warehouse via JDBC.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 5  
**BRD References**: NFR-5.4 (Snowflake), clarification Q-B2  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Daily ELT job runs at 04:00 UTC; exports previous day's production aggregates and allocation records
- [ ] Only finalized allocation data exported; draft/in-progress periods excluded
- [ ] Job failure retried automatically (3 attempts); IT Ops alerted on final failure
- [ ] Export schema documented and versioned  
**Dependencies**: EPIC-003 (allocation records as source)

---

## EPIC-002: Well Test Management

### FEAT-007: Desktop Well Test Submission Form
**Epic**: EPIC-002 — Well Test Management  
**Description**: A web-based form for field operators and production engineers to submit well test records including test date, duration, choke size, separator readings, and stabilisation confirmation, with immediate auto-calculation of production rates and fluid ratios.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-010, FR-011, BR-012, WF-003  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All required fields (date, duration, choke size, oil/gas/water separator readings, stabilisation tick) enforced
- [ ] Minimum duration validation: ≥ 6h for oil wells, ≥ 4h for gas wells; rejected with clear error (BR-012)
- [ ] Test rates (BOPD, MMSCFD, BWPD) and GOR/WC auto-calculated on submission
- [ ] Submitted test record is immutable; rejection creates new linked record (clarification Q-C5)
- [ ] Operator receives confirmation with calculated rates before final submit  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-026 (auth)

### FEAT-008: Offline-first Mobile Well Test App
**Epic**: EPIC-002 — Well Test Management  
**Description**: React Native mobile app (iOS 17+ / Android 14+) that allows field operators to create and submit well test records without network connectivity, storing submissions locally in WatermelonDB and syncing automatically within 60 seconds of connectivity restore.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-012, AC-001, WF-003  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Well test form fully functional with no network connection
- [ ] Pending submissions queue visible to operator with sync status (pending / synced / failed)
- [ ] App automatically syncs queued submissions within 60 seconds of network reconnect (AC-001)
- [ ] Reference data (well list, choke size lookup) cached on device and refreshed on sync
- [ ] Sync conflict resolution: server rejects duplicate; operator notified
- [ ] App works on iOS 17+ and Android 14+ devices enrolled in Intune MDM  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-007 (shared validation logic), FEAT-026 (Azure AD mobile auth)

### FEAT-009: Well Test Two-Level Approval Workflow
**Epic**: EPIC-002 — Well Test Management  
**Description**: Implements the two-level approval chain for submitted well tests: Level 1 (Production Engineer) is always required; Level 2 (Lead Production Engineer) is triggered automatically when the test rate deviates more than 20% from the previous approved test.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-013, BR-013, WF-003  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Submitted well test creates an L1 approval task for the responsible Production Engineer
- [ ] System automatically calculates deviation from prior approved test; if > 20%, L2 task created after L1 approval
- [ ] Each approver can approve or reject with mandatory comment
- [ ] Rejection immutable; new test submission required
- [ ] Approver receives in-app notification and email notification for pending tasks
- [ ] Approved test triggers async test ratio update in allocation engine within 24 hours (BR-009)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-007, FEAT-008

### FEAT-010: Well Test Validity Tracking
**Epic**: EPIC-002 — Well Test Management  
**Description**: Tracks the validity expiry of each approved well test (30 days for oil producers, 60 days for gas injectors) and surfaces expiring tests as warnings and expired tests as allocation blockers.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 5  
**BRD References**: BR-016, BR-004, FR-009  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Well test validity expiry calculated and stored at approval time (oil: +30 days, gas: +60 days)
- [ ] Dashboard widget shows wells with tests expiring within 7 days (configurable warning threshold)
- [ ] Expired test prevents monthly allocation finalization for the affected allocation group (BR-016)
- [ ] VP Operations approval exception can override expiry block; approval logged in system  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-009

---

## EPIC-003: Production Allocation Engine

### FEAT-011: Well Test Ratio Allocation Calculation
**Epic**: EPIC-003 — Production Allocation Engine  
**Description**: Implements the primary allocation method: distributing measured separator volumes to individual commingled wells using approved well test ratios, storing both gross separator volumes and stock tank barrel equivalents per well.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, BR-005, BR-009, WF-004  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Allocation formula applied: `Well_i = (Test_i / ΣTest) × Sep_Vol` for all wells in each allocation group
- [ ] Gross separator and stock tank volumes (post shrinkage factor) stored per well per allocation record
- [ ] Allocation methodology selectable per allocation point (well test ratio / back-allocation / VFM stub)
- [ ] VFM stub returns HTTP 501 with "Phase 2" message; UI option greyed out (clarification Q-C7)
- [ ] Test ratio update reflected in the engine within 24 hours of test approval (BR-009)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-009 (approved test ratios), FEAT-010 (validity checks)

### FEAT-012: Monthly Allocation Finalization Workflow
**Epic**: EPIC-003 — Production Allocation Engine  
**Description**: Orchestrates the end-of-month production allocation process: runs calculations, enforces all three BR-011 finalization blockers, routes for Production Engineer and Production Accountant approval, and locks the period.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-009, BR-004, BR-011, WF-004, AC-002  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Allocation calculation for 340 wells runs in under 90 seconds (NFR-002)
- [ ] Finalization blocked and blockers listed if: any well has missing/expired test, allocation vs measured diff > ±0.5% at any allocation group, any unresolved Communication Loss event exists
- [ ] Production Engineer can view and remediate each blocker individually
- [ ] Production Accountant receives certification task after Production Engineer submits
- [ ] Period locked after Production Accountant certification; no changes permitted
- [ ] Monthly allocation report available within 15 minutes of month-end midnight (AC-002)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-011, FEAT-003

### FEAT-013: Retroactive Allocation Correction
**Epic**: EPIC-003 — Production Allocation Engine  
**Description**: Allows Production Engineers to correct past allocation periods (up to 90 days prior) with appropriate dual-approval for corrections older than 30 days, full change log, and automatic flagging of affected regulatory reports.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-008, BR-006, BR-010, WF-007  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Retroactive corrections accepted for periods up to 90 days prior; older periods rejected (Exc-3 in WF-007)
- [ ] Corrections ≤ 30 days require Lead Production Engineer approval only
- [ ] Corrections > 30 days require both Lead Production Engineer and Production Accountant approval
- [ ] Every retroactive change logged: change reason, approver identities, timestamp, old and new values
- [ ] If the corrected period was used in a submitted regulatory report, system flags for amendment  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-012

### FEAT-014: Allocation Report & Summary View
**Epic**: EPIC-003 — Production Allocation Engine  
**Description**: Provides a full-featured allocation report screen showing daily and monthly well-level volumes, methodology used, allocation group totals, and the reconciliation check between allocated and measured volumes.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-009, FR-005, BR-005, AC-003  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Report displays well-level oil/gas/water/condensate volumes (gross + stock tank) per day and monthly total
- [ ] Allocation group reconciliation percentage displayed (allocated vs measured; target ≤ ±0.5%)
- [ ] ESTIMATED-flagged data clearly marked in the report
- [ ] Report filterable by well, platform/field, date range
- [ ] Data exportable to CSV  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-012

---

## EPIC-004: Downtime & Loss Accounting

### FEAT-015: Downtime Event Logging
**Epic**: EPIC-004 — Downtime & Loss Accounting  
**Description**: Provides the UI and API for field operators and production engineers to create, update, and close downtime events with the full category taxonomy, enforcing comment requirements for the "Unclassified" category.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-014, FR-015, BR-018, WF-005  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Downtime event form captures: well ID, start datetime, end datetime (or "Ongoing"), all 8 categories and sub-categories, freetext comment
- [ ] "Unclassified" category enforces non-empty freetext comment before save (BR-018)
- [ ] "Ongoing" events visible on well status; closeable with actual end datetime
- [ ] Production Engineer can edit category/sub-category after Field Operator submission
- [ ] SAP PM maintenance events linked when category is "Planned maintenance" or "Well intervention"  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-026 (RBAC + asset scoping)

### FEAT-016: Deferred Production Calculation
**Epic**: EPIC-004 — Downtime & Loss Accounting  
**Description**: Automatically calculates deferred production (in BBL and MMSCF) for each downtime event using the approved well test rate active at the time of the event, and recalculates if the event's start/end times are edited.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 5  
**BRD References**: FR-016, BR-017  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Deferred BBL = `Test_Rate_BOPD × Downtime_Hours / 24` using test rate at event start time
- [ ] Deferred MMSCF calculated in parallel for gas wells
- [ ] Recalculated automatically on event close (actual end time replaces estimated) and on category edit
- [ ] Deferred production visible on event record and in monthly downtime report  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-015, FEAT-009 (approved test rates)

### FEAT-017: Monthly Downtime Report Export
**Epic**: EPIC-004 — Downtime & Loss Accounting  
**Description**: Generates and exports the monthly downtime summary report, summarised by well, category, and platform/field, in both Excel (.xlsx) and PDF formats.  
**Priority**: Should Have  
**Total Story Points**: 5  
**BRD References**: FR-017  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Report covers a complete calendar month (selectable)
- [ ] Rows grouped by platform/field, then well, with subtotals per downtime category
- [ ] Deferred production shown per event and as monthly well/field totals
- [ ] Excel export preserves formatting and formulas for field totals
- [ ] PDF export renders tables correctly without truncation  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-016

---

## EPIC-005: Regulatory Reporting & Submission

### FEAT-018: BSEE MMS-146 Report Generation
**Epic**: EPIC-005 — Regulatory Reporting & Submission  
**Description**: Auto-populates the BSEE Form MMS-146 (Monthly Production Report) for each offshore lease from finalized allocation data, using the April 2025 schema, and presents the pre-populated report for Regulatory Analyst review before submission.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-018, WF-006  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] MMS-146 populated from finalized allocations (FR-018) immediately on period finalization
- [ ] All fields mapped per April 2025 BSEE schema
- [ ] Report UI shows field-by-field values with source (well, allocation group) for traceability
- [ ] Regulatory Analyst can view but not directly edit auto-populated fields (edits require allocation correction → WF-007)
- [ ] Comparison to prior submission displayed to aid review  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-012 (finalized allocations required)

### FEAT-019: Texas RRC PR-2 Report Generation & Submission
**Epic**: EPIC-005 — Regulatory Reporting & Submission  
**Description**: Generates the Texas RRC PR-2 Purchaser Report for all Texas onshore wells from finalized allocations, maps to RRC v3.2 electronic filing schema, and submits via the RRC OAuth2 REST API.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-020, clarification Q-D3  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] PR-2 data correctly mapped to all RRC v3.2 schema fields for Texas onshore wells
- [ ] OAuth2 token obtained via client credentials; secret stored in Azure Key Vault
- [ ] Submission to RRC REST API returns success; confirmation reference stored in system
- [ ] Failed submission retried up to 3 times before alerting Regulatory Analyst  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-012

### FEAT-020: BSEE MMS-146 SFTP Submission & Report Locking
**Epic**: EPIC-005 — Regulatory Reporting & Submission  
**Description**: Submits the approved BSEE MMS-146 CSV file to the eFAST SFTP portal, locks the report record against further editing, and stores the submission confirmation reference.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-021, BR-014, WF-006  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] CSV file generated per MMS-146 BSEE fixed-column layout; placed in eFAST SFTP drop folder
- [ ] SFTP transfer confirmation stored against the report record
- [ ] Report locked (read-only) immediately after successful SFTP upload (BR-014)
- [ ] Amendment workflow creates new versioned amendment record with mandatory justification; original locked record preserved  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-018

### FEAT-021: Regulatory Deadline Reminders
**Epic**: EPIC-005 — Regulatory Reporting & Submission  
**Description**: Automated scheduled notifications sent to the Regulatory Affairs Analyst and VP Operations at 10 days and 3 days before the BSEE 15th-of-month submission deadline if the report has not yet been submitted.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 3  
**BRD References**: FR-019, BR-019  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Email sent 10 days before the 15th if BSEE report for the previous month is not in "Submitted" status
- [ ] Email sent 3 days before the 15th with same condition
- [ ] Emails sent to Regulatory Analyst and VP Operations (configurable recipient list)
- [ ] No reminder sent if report is already in "Submitted" or "Locked" status
- [ ] Email content includes report period, deadline date, and link to report review screen  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-018

## EPIC-006: Production Dashboards & Analytics

### FEAT-022: Real-time Production KPI Dashboard
**Epic**: EPIC-006 — Production Dashboards & Analytics  
**Description**: The primary landing screen for Production Engineers and leadership showing real-time production KPIs: total field production, on-stream efficiency per platform/field, top producing and most-deferred wells, and well count by status.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-022, NFR-001, WF-008 (feeds alert display)  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All KPIs (BOPD, MMSCFD, BWPD, OSE, top 5 producing, top 5 deferred, well counts) displayed on page load
- [ ] All KPIs refresh within 10 seconds of page open (NFR-001); powered by Redis cache + ADX KQL
- [ ] Dashboard filterable by platform/field
- [ ] Well count by status tile shows: producing, shut-in, injection, workover counts
- [ ] "Top 5 deferred" defaults to month-to-date; toggle to rolling 30-day view available (clarification Q-C2)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001, FEAT-004, FEAT-016 (OSE and deferred data), FEAT-026 (auth)

### FEAT-023: Production Anomaly Detection & Alert Workflows
**Epic**: EPIC-006 — Production Dashboards & Analytics  
**Description**: Background monitoring service that evaluates real-time production data against three alert thresholds (over-rate, high WC, GOR breach), generates alert records, notifies engineers, and tracks alert resolution workflows.  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 13  
**BRD References**: FR-022, BR-001, BR-002, BR-003, WF-008  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Over-rate alert (BR-001): generated when well > 15% above test rate for 3 consecutive days; sent to responsible Production Engineer
- [ ] High WC alert (BR-002): generated when oil producer WC > 95%; "High Water Cut" event created; Reservoir Engineer notified
- [ ] GOR breach alert (BR-003): generated when GOR > 125% of approved reservoir GOR; review workflow created; cannot be closed without Reservoir Engineer note
- [ ] All alert types appear in dashboard alert feed with status (open/acknowledged/resolved)
- [ ] Anomaly detected and alert generated within 30 minutes of threshold breach (BO-004)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001, FEAT-009 (approved test rates for over-rate calculation)

### FEAT-024: Well Decline Curve Visualization
**Epic**: EPIC-006 — Production Dashboards & Analytics  
**Description**: Well-level production history chart overlaid with the approved type curve, allowing reservoir engineers to visually assess production performance against prediction.  
**Priority**: Should Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: FR-024, clarification Q-C6  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Chart shows historical daily production rate (BOPD or MMSCFD) overlaid with type curve line
- [ ] Type curve data sourced from most recently imported CSV for the well (uploaded by Reservoir Engineer)
- [ ] Chart navigable by date range; minimum 24-month history displayed
- [ ] Toggle between oil rate (BOPD) and gas rate (MMSCFD) views
- [ ] ESTIMATED-flagged data points shown with visual distinction (dashed or different colour)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-001, FEAT-047 (type curve import — part of FEAT-028 admin)

### FEAT-025: Chart & Data Export
**Epic**: EPIC-006 — Production Dashboards & Analytics  
**Description**: Enables users to export any dashboard chart as a PNG image and the underlying dataset as a CSV file for use in reports and presentations.  
**Priority**: Should Have  
**Total Story Points**: 3  
**BRD References**: FR-023, FR-017  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Every chart panel has an "Export PNG" button; downloads chart at screen resolution
- [ ] Every chart panel has an "Export CSV" button; downloads tabular data backing the chart
- [ ] CSV columns include: date, well ID, value, unit, data quality flag
- [ ] Export works for all chart types: KPI tiles, time-series, decline curves, downtime summaries  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-022, FEAT-024

## EPIC-007: Platform Foundation — Security, Audit & Administration

### FEAT-026: Azure AD SSO & Role-based Access Control
**Epic**: EPIC-007 — Platform Foundation  
**Description**: Integrates WPMA with Azure Active Directory via SAML 2.0 for authentication, and implements seven-role RBAC with asset-scope filtering so that each user sees only the data their role and field assignment authorise.  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Effort**: M  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: NFR-004, NFR-005, clarification Q-A with auth, Q-C4, Q-E1  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All users authenticated via Azure AD SAML 2.0; no local username/password accounts exist
- [ ] Seven roles enforced: Field Operator, Production Engineer, Lead Engineer, Production Accountant, Regulatory Analyst, Read-Only, System Administrator
- [ ] Users may hold multiple roles (additive permissions), except System Administrator (exclusive)
- [ ] Field Operators see only wells/events for their assigned platform/field (asset-scope filter)
- [ ] Unauthorized access to an endpoint or data record returns HTTP 403  
**Dependencies**: None (foundation feature — must be first)

### FEAT-027: Immutable Audit Trail
**Epic**: EPIC-007 — Platform Foundation  
**Description**: Captures every create/update/delete action on production-critical data (allocations, well tests, downtime events, regulatory records) into an immutable audit log with 7-year compliance retention, using PostgreSQL hot storage (2 years) and Azure Blob cold archive (years 3–7).  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: NFR-007, AC-005, clarification Q-E2  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Every data modification on allocation, well test, downtime, and regulatory records logged with: entity type, entity ID, user ID, action, old value (JSON), new value (JSON), ISO-8601 timestamp
- [ ] Audit log records are immutable; UPDATE and DELETE on audit_log table are denied at DB level
- [ ] System Administrator can query audit log by entity, user, and date range (2-year hot store)
- [ ] Records older than 2 years automatically moved to Blob Storage (lifecycle policy)
- [ ] 100% of production/allocation changes captured during acceptance test period (AC-005)  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-026

### FEAT-028: System Administration Console
**Epic**: EPIC-007 — Platform Foundation  
**Description**: Provides System Administrators with tools to manage well master data, assign users to roles and asset scopes, import type curves for wells, and configure system integration settings (PI endpoint, DeltaV, SAP, RRC credentials).  
**Priority**: Must Have  
**Total Story Points**: 8  
**BRD References**: NFR-005, clarification Q-C6 (type curve import), Q-D3 (RRC credential config)  
**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] System Administrator can create, edit, and deactivate well master records (well ID, name, type, platform/field, commodity, shrinkage factor)
- [ ] System Administrator can assign users to roles and asset scopes (platform/field)
- [ ] Reservoir Engineer can upload type curve CSV per well; system validates format and stores
- [ ] Integration settings (PI API URL/credentials, DeltaV OPC-UA endpoint, SAP BAPI connection, RRC OAuth client ID/secret, BSEE SFTP credentials) managed via admin UI; secrets written to Azure Key Vault
- [ ] Allocation point configuration (allocation group → wells mapping, methodology selection) managed by System Administrator  
**Dependencies**: FEAT-026
